target.title: feat(data): add bounded data run evidence
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `crabbox data` as a thin wrapper over `crabbox run` for repo-owned data workflows with required bounded manifests.
- Validate `crabbox.data-run.v1` manifests locally, reject unsafe/unknown proof fields and unproven `enforced` policy claims, write local summaries, and attach bounded `dataSummary` metadata to coordinator run records when available.
- Add provider-neutral delegated artifact retrieval plus Islo single-file support for `--require-artifact` / `--download`, while keeping globs and unsupported delegated providers fail-closed.
- Split delegated archive artifact collection from local single-file download materialization so providers like Blacksmith remain fail-closed for `--download` until they implement that exact capability.
- Add provider-neutral data-run policy hooks for source identity, sink identity, egress, and promotion declarations. Islo participates as `declared-only`; `enforced` remains reserved for adapters that can prove enforcement.
- Harden data-run local output containment, Worker broker metadata sanitization, and Islo retrieval against symlink, oversize, unsafe proof-key, and archive-chunk log-leak edge cases.

## Issue Coverage
| Issue | Status in this PR | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/225 | Implemented as product framing | Keeps the feature framed as generic run evidence over caller-owned commands and bounded proof. |
| https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/227 | Implemented for first provider | Adds the provider-neutral delegated download capability and proves it on Islo. Other delegated providers remain fail-closed until adapters implement it. |
| https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/228 | Implemented | Adds bounded manifest validation, local summaries, and coordinator run-record `dataSummary` storage with Worker-side sanitization. |
| https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/229 | Implemented | Adds the thin `crabbox data run` wrapper over existing run primitives. |
| https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/230 | Implemented as declaration/hook layer | Adds provider-neutral policy hooks and explicit enforcement tiers. This PR does not claim provider-enforced identity/egress/promotion where an adapter cannot prove it. |

## Test plan
- `go test ./internal/cli ./internal/providers/islo ./internal/providers/blacksmith`
- `go test ./...`
- `go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.45.0 -test ./...`
- `npm ci --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `scripts/check-docs.sh`
- `rg` and `ast-grep` sweeps over delegated artifact/download capabilities

## Fresh Live Islo E2E Proof
This was run from PR head `eafef3a`. A short-lived API key was created through `islo api-key create --expires 1 --output-file ...`, used only via `CRABBOX_ISLO_API_KEY`, then deleted. Lease/sandbox IDs are redacted.

```text
ISLO_LIVE_HEAD eafef3a
ISLO_LIVE_STATUS 0
ISLO_LIVE_PROOF_BEGIN
data policy source=declared-live-smoke sink=declared-live-smoke egress=provider-default promotion=manual enforcement=declared-only
leased <redacted> slug=<redacted> provider=islo sandbox=<redacted>
provider=islo lease=<redacted> sandbox=<redacted>
required artifact reports/data/manifest.json matched=1
required artifact reports/data/empty.marker matched=1
downloaded .crabbox/data-runs/islo-live-proof/manifest.json bytes=405
islo run summary sync_skipped=true command=470ms total=51.459s exit=0
data run islo-live-proof manifest ok status=success inputs=0 outputs=1 summary=.crabbox/data-runs/islo-live-proof/summary.json
islo-live-proof manifest=reports/data/manifest.json provider=islo target=- policy=source=declared-live-smoke sink=declared-live-smoke egress=provider-default promotion=manual enforcement=declared-only
ISLO_LIVE_PROOF_END
```

## Provider Scope
- Islo is the delegated provider proven live in this PR for bounded single-file required artifacts and downloads.
- Blacksmith keeps its bounded archive/glob artifact path and remains fail-closed for `--download` until it implements single-file materialization.
- W&B/CoreWeave remains on its existing delegated-run contract and still rejects artifact retrieval flags in this PR.
- `enforced` data-run policy claims are reserved for adapters that can prove enforcement; this PR supports `declared-only` and `unsupported` and exposes the provider hook for future enforcement-capable adapters.
target.author: zozo123
